rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assign bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and component ports in AGREE expressions only when they are explicitly declared in the same scope.
Define constants and equations in component type annexes for reusable values and logical relations.
Place implementation-level assignments in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections are defined.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for contracts.
Equations and constants are declared with `eq name : type = expression;` and `const name : type = value;`.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for assign clauses.
Do not reference AADL timing properties or unit tokens as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic scope in AGREE
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
